description: A man carrying a briefcase walks briskly across a rehearsal stage with theater seating in the background. "This is a terrible play," he says.
The man continues walking and exits through a doorway, saying, "I'll see you in the morning."
Audience laughter is audible. On the stage, Joey and Kate stand near a small table. Kate presses a hand to her forehead.
Lowering her hand to her chin, Kate says, "I can't believe we go on in a week."
Kate looks at Joey, her hand dropping slightly.
Kate says, "It's gonna be alright."
Joey and Kate stand in silence.
Lauren walks onto the stage carrying a bag. Approaching Joey, she says, "Hey, so since we're getting off early, do you want to go and paint mugs?"
Joey looks at Lauren with his mouth slightly open, turning his body toward her.
Joey asks, "What?"
Lauren maintains eye contact with Joey.
Gesturing with one hand, Lauren says, "You know, at the place I told you about last night."
Joey points at Lauren and says, "It oh, yeah, with the mug painting."
Joey nods, saying, "Yeah, I was so listening to that." Audience laughter sounds.
Joey lowers his hand and shifts his posture.
Gesturing with his hands, Joey says, "But you know what? I think I kind of need to work on my stuff tonight."
Lauren smiles and says, "Oh, okay. I'll see you tomorrow."
Joey leans in and kisses Lauren briefly. They part, and she turns and walks toward the stairs.
Joey says, "Okay," while looking after her.
Lauren continues up the stairs.
Lauren says, "Night," as she walks up the stairs.
Kate sits on the edge of the small table. Joey turns around, exhales audibly, and runs both hands through his hair.
Joey lowers his hands and steps toward Kate, maintaining his gaze on her.
Joey asks, "Are you okay?"
Kate inhales and adjusts her posture.
Looking up at him, Kate replies, "Yeah, I guess."
Kate shifts her gaze away, bringing one hand up to her neck.
Standing up, Kate gestures and says, "Like, what are we going to do about the, huh?" Joey shakes his head and adds, "I don't"
Kate lowers her hands slightly.
Kate crosses her arms and says, "Well, um,"
Joey looks at Kate as she keeps her arms crossed.
Gesturing with both hands, Kate says, "Maybe if it had more."
Joey keeps his gaze fixed on Kate.
Joey asks, "Well, how do you mean?"
Kate replies, "Well, Adrian's looking for a reason to stay, right?"
Pressing her fists to her stomach, Kate says, "Victor can't just kiss her."
Joey raises a finger while looking at Kate.
Joey points at Kate and says, "He's got to really give her a reason. You know, maybe he could slip her the tongue." Audience laughter sounds.
Kate raises her hands slightly, keeping her fists clenched, and stares at Joey.
Kate tightens her grip and lowers her hands, maintaining her gaze on Joey.
Kate says, "Or maybe"
Kate uncrosses her arms and steps forward.
Stepping closer and reaching out to grip Joey's arms, Kate says, "He could grab her and lift her up."
Joey gestures actively and says, "Yeah, and then Adrian, maybe she could wrap her legs around his waist."
Kate nods repeatedly, maintaining eye contact with Joey.
Kate says, "She could do that." Joey gestures with his hands and adds, "Yeah, and claw at his back."
Kate maintains her gaze on Joey.
Kate points at Joey and says, "Yeah, right."
Kate gestures near her own chest and stomach, saying, "And then she could rip off his shirt and kiss his chest and his stomach."
Joey points at his own teeth and says, "And then he can use his teeth. His teeth to undo her dress and"
Kate stares at Joey with wide eyes.
Kate says, "bite her."
Kate grips her fists tightly in front of her, saying, "And then right when the scene ends, he could take her with this raw animal."
The setting changes to a bedroom with dim lighting. Joey and Kate lie in bed under the covers, tossing and turning rapidly as blankets rustle.
Joey and Kate stop moving and lie still side by side, both breathing heavily.
Joey looks at Kate and says, "Something like that."
Kate turns her head toward Joey.
Kate replies, "Yeah, that's pretty much what I had in mind," and Joey adds, "Yeah."
The setting changes to a city street corner during the day, with cars driving by and people walking on the sidewalk.
The setting changes to an apartment kitchen. Joey enters through the door, smiling and holding his hands out as audience laughter sounds.
Joey walks over to Monica, who holds a mug at the table, and hugs her from behind. Ross sits nearby eating.
Joey releases Monica, pulls up a chair, and leans over to wrap his arms around Ross's neck in a hug.
Ross stops eating, his eyes widening slightly as Joey holds the hug. Audience laughter continues.
Ross touches Joey's arm and says, "It's a little early to be"
Joey begins to release his grip on Ross.
Joey releases Ross, standing up and saying, "No, no. Things finally happened with Kate."
Monica smiles and says, "You're kidding."
Monica says, "It's great." Joey gestures with his hands and adds, "It was so amazing."
Joey pulls back a chair.
Joey sits down, saying, "After the love making." Monica reaches for an item on the table and says, "Oh my"
Monica handles a container on the table while Joey sits beside Ross, looking forward.
Joey gestures to his own chest, raising and lowering his hand as he says, "I just watched her sleep for like hours, just breathing in and breathing out."
Joey keeps his hand on his chest.
Joey points to his eyes and says, "And then I knew she was dreaming because her eyes kept going like this."
Joey repeatedly flutters his eyes while keeping them closed. Ross and Monica watch him as audience laughter sounds.
In an apartment kitchen, Monica holds a mug. Chandler walks through the open front door, followed by Rachel. Ross and Joey sit at the kitchen table.

Chandler walks forward and gestures with both hands, saying, "I'm telling you, Joanna's got it all wrong, okay? All I said was this was fun. Let's do it again sometime. I'll give you a call." Rachel turns to face him, while Joey, Monica, and Ross watch.
Rachel crosses her arms slightly and says, "Oh, gee. I wonder why she thinks you're going to call her," her volume slightly raised. Chandler looks at her.
Chandler steps closer to Rachel.

Chandler points to his own face and says, "That's what you say at the end of a date. You can't just say, Nice to meet you, good night to her face," pitching his voice higher on the quoted phrase. Rachel looks at him.
Joey leans forward slightly at the kitchen table.
Joey, smiling, says, "I'm so happy," while looking at Chandler. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler raises his hands, palms facing forward toward Joey, then turns back to Rachel.
Chandler gestures as he says, "Look, it's the end of the date. I'm standing there, I know. All she's waiting for is for me to say I'll call her and it"
Chandler drops his hands and continues, "just comes out. I can't help it. It's a compulsion." Rachel listens.
Rachel turns her head toward Joey at the table.
Joey says, "Come on, Rachel. When a guy says he's going to call, it doesn't mean he's going to call," gesturing toward Monica.
Monica looks at Joey, holding a spoon in her hand.
Monica asks, "This never happened to you?"
Rachel looks down briefly before meeting Monica's gaze and replies, "Well, they always called."
Monica looks at Rachel, drawing her eyebrows together.
Monica raises her mug toward her face and says, "Bite me," followed by audience laughter.
Rachel turns away. The scene changes to a city street view with traffic moving on an elevated roadway.
The setting changes to an office. Rachel sits at a desk typing on a laptop, and a brown-haired woman, Sophie, sits at an adjacent desk. Joanna enters the office holding files.
Joanna approaches Rachel's desk and asks, "Did he call?"
Rachel shakes her head slightly and says, "No, sorry."
Joanna asks, "Why?"

Joanna drops the files on a desk and says, "He said he'd call. Why hasn't he called? Maybe he's intimidated by really smart, strong, successful women." Sophie leans forward and nods. Joanna turns to her and says, "Sophie, will you please"
Joanna finishes, "climb out of my butt?" Audience laughter sounds.
Sophie leans back and rests her chin on her hand. Joanna turns back to Rachel.
Joanna asks, "Why hasn't he called?"
Rachel stammers slightly, looking down at her laptop and raising her hands with her palms facing outward.
Rachel says, "Maybe he maybe he feels awkward because you were my boss."
Joanna crosses her arms.
Joanna gestures toward Rachel and says, "Awkward? Why should he feel awkward? Well, the only person that should feel awkward is you. And you didn't tell him not to call me, did you?"
Rachel says, "No." Joanna gestures continuously and says, "Because if you feel uncomfortable with your friend dating someone you work for, there are always ways to fix that."
Rachel nods slowly, keeping her hands clasped together.
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Chandler sits on a sofa. Rachel walks up to him rapidly, holding a magazine, and says, "Call her. Call her now."
Chandler leans back on the sofa, looking up at Rachel.
Chandler says, "Multiple," followed by audience laughter.
Chandler gestures with his hands, palms up, "So many paper cuts." Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel stares at Chandler, her lips pressed together.
Rachel asks, "Why hasn't he called, Rachel?" her pitch higher as she mimics another voice.
Rachel paces slightly and continues in the higher pitch, "Why? Why? I don't understand why? He said he'd why? Why?"

Rachel points at Chandler, her voice returning to normal, and says, "Chandler, I'm telling you, she has flipped out. She's gone crazy." Chandler leans forward, extends his hand, and replies, "Oh, well, give me the phone then."
Rachel places the magazine into her bag.
Rachel gestures emphatically and says, "This isn't funny. She thinks it's my fault that you haven't called her. You have to call her. Look, you can't call"
Chandler stands up and says, "somebody after this long just to say in case you didn't notice, I don't like you." Audience laughter sounds.
Rachel replies, "Well, then you're going to have to take her out again."

Chandler shakes his head and says, "No. She's really dull, and she gets this gross mascara goop thing in the corner of her eye," bringing his finger to the corner of his eye. Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel groans, placing her hand on her forehead, and then turns back to Chandler. Audience laughter sounds.
Rachel gestures continually and says, "I don't care. I don't care. You're going to have to take her out again and end it and end it in a way that she knows"
Rachel continues, "it's actually ended. And I don't care how hard it is for you."
Rachel says, "Do not tell her that you will call her again." Chandler points at her and replies, "All right, fine. But it's just a lunch date. No more than an hour. And from now"
Chandler continues, "on, I get my own dates. I don't want you setting me up with anybody ever again." Rachel says, "That's fine."
Chandler looks at Rachel, his arms relaxed.
Chandler points at Rachel and says, "That's just a lot of big talk, you know?" Rachel replies, "I know." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler turns and walks away. The setting changes to the apartment living room. Ross opens the front door and walks in.
Ross calls out, "Mon," and a female voice replies off-screen, "In the shower."
Ross walks into the kitchen and approaches the refrigerator.
Ross pulls a container from the refrigerator. "It," is heard off-screen.
Ross closes the refrigerator holding the container, then turns toward the kitchen table.
A cardboard house model rests on the table, emitting smoke from its roof.
Ross runs to the table, sets the container down, and repeatedly waves his hands over the model as flames erupt from its roof. Audience laughter sounds.
In a kitchen, a cardboard dollhouse on a table is engulfed in flames. Ross quickly turns to the sink, grabs a clear glass, and fills it with water.
Ross turns back to the table and splashes the water onto the burning dollhouse, causing the flames to diminish slightly.
Ross waves his hands rapidly, fills the glass at the sink again, and throws more water onto the dollhouse as thick smoke rises.
Ross places the glass down, grabs the smoking, partially burning dollhouse with both hands, and walks quickly toward the apartment door.
Ross opens the apartment door and exits into the hallway, carrying the smoking dollhouse in front of him.
In the hallway, Ross shouts, drops the burning dollhouse to the floor, and runs out of view. Audience laughter is audible.
The scene changes to an empty theater auditorium. Joey sits in the front row of red seats, facing the aisle.
Kate walks down the aisle steps toward Joey, carrying a dark bag over her shoulder.
Joey stands up as Kate stops near him. "So I talked to Lauren. Kind of told her how things were with us," he says.
Kate sets her bag down on a seat and looks at Joey.
"Did you talk to Marshall?" Kate asks. "About what?" Joey responds.
Kate raises her eyebrows and looks closely at Joey.
"You know, about what happened with us?" Kate says, holding her hands out.
Joey stares back at her.
"No. And there's really no reason why you should find out so not make a big deal of it, okay?" Kate says, gesturing with a rolled-up script in her hand.
Joey turns away from her and walks toward the front of the stage.
Joey turns back to face her, gesturing with both hands. "What are you talking about? It was a big deal," he says.
Joey walks over to the stage and sits on its edge. Kate walks onto the stage and sits on a sofa facing him.
"Come on, you can't tell me last night didn't mean something to you. I was there. You're not that good an actress," Joey says, leaning forward with his hands open.
Kate looks back at him.
"It was. I was just caught up in the moment," Kate says, gesturing with her hands.
Joey keeps his gaze fixed on her.
"That's all it was," Kate adds.
Joey lowers his head.
Kate leans forward slightly. "Joey, I'm sorry you feel bad but haven't you ever slept with a woman where it meant more to her than it did to you?" she asks.
Joey maintains eye contact with her.
"No," Joey says, shaking his head.
Lauren walks down the aisle steps behind Joey, approaching them.
"Hi, Kate," Lauren says. "Hi, Lauren," Kate replies.
Lauren stops next to Joey, holding a water bottle. Joey looks up at her.
"Hi, Lauren," Joey says. "Hi, pig," Lauren replies.
Lauren drinks from the water bottle and walks past him. Joey lowers his head, while Kate looks on from the stage.
The scene returns to Monica's kitchen. The dollhouse sits on the table, heavily charred. Monica, wearing a bathrobe, stands nearby while Ross holds a piece of the structure.
"Sorry I scared you in there," Ross says. "Oh, it's okay," Monica replies.
Ross examines the small object in his hand, placing it back on the table.
"By the way, I was just checking the shower massager," Ross says, gesturing with his hands.
"Yeah," Ross adds. Audience laughter sounds.
Phoebe opens the apartment door and walks inside.
Phoebe walks up to Monica and places her hands on her shoulders. "Hey. Tried to reach you at work," she says.
Monica places her hands on Phoebe's arms.
"There's been a fire," Monica says.
"What? Oh, my God," Phoebe says, gasping and bringing her hands to her mouth.
Phoebe turns and walks quickly toward the kitchen table, looking at the charred dollhouse.
"What happened?" Phoebe asks. "Well, we believe it originated here," Ross answers, pointing to a section of the house.
Ross leans over the house, keeping his hand pointed at it.
"In the Aroma Room," Ross continues.
Phoebe looks closely at the dollhouse.
"All right, did everyone get out okay?" Phoebe asks Monica.
"What?" Monica asks. "The draft's okay," Phoebe replies, picking up a small object from the table.
"And so is the pirate," Phoebe adds, holding up the objects.
Phoebe picks up another small, charred object from the table.
"What is this?" Phoebe asks. "No, Phoebe, don't look. You don't want to see what's under there," Ross says, raising his hand.
Phoebe holds the object up to her face and smells it.
"The foster puppets," Phoebe says, her voice breaking, then coughs.
Phoebe coughs loudly and brings both hands to her face, visibly crying. Monica places a hand on Phoebe's back.
The scene changes to an exterior daytime view of city buildings.
Inside an office, Chandler points to his nose while standing near a desk. "It's not a big deal," he says.
"It's just it's right here, and it's all the time," Chandler says, continuing to gesture near his face.
Joanna stands opposite Chandler, listening and nodding.
Chandler turns slightly toward the door. "Well, thanks again for lunch," he says.
Joanna follows Chandler as he moves toward the door, past Rachel who sits at a desk outside.
"Yes, this was pleasant," Joanna says. "It was, wasn't it?" Chandler replies.
Chandler stands with his hands in his pockets.
"The food there was great, wasn't it?" Chandler says.
Joanna smiles at him.
"So take care," Chandler says.
Chandler looks at Joanna.
"You too," Joanna replies.
Chandler takes a step backward out of the office.
Chandler walks backward through the doorway, waving his arms. "This was great. I'll give you a call. We should do it again sometime. I'm looking forward to it," he says rapidly.
Joanna turns to Rachel. "Rachel, any messages?" she asks.
"Sophie's death?" Rachel replies, looking up from her desk.
Joanna walks away from Rachel's desk, while Rachel watches her go.
In a brightly lit office hallway, Rachel stands near a doorway, pushing Chandler backward.
Rachel asks, "Chandler, are you going to call her?" Chandler covers his eyes with one hand and says, "No." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel says, "Chandler." Chandler lowers his hand, holding his arms out with palms open, and says, "Look, I'm sorry, okay? I'm weak and pathetic and sorry." Laughter continues.
Rachel points her finger at Chandler's chest and says, "Okay, you are going to tell her. You are going to tell her," while pushing him toward the open doorway.
Rachel finishes, "...now." She shoves Chandler into the office, where Joanna sits on a desk holding papers. Chandler shouts, "I'm not going to call you," as laughter plays.
Joanna lowers the papers and looks toward Chandler. Chandler stands near the doorway, shifting his weight.
Joanna asks, "What's?" Chandler steps further into the room, making a quick hopping motion.
Chandler says, "I'm..." Audience laughter occurs.
Chandler points toward Joanna and continues, "...sorry. I'm sorry that I said I was going to when I'm not."
Lowering his hand, he adds, "I was just trying to be nice and you don't deserve that." Audience laughter is audible.
Joanna looks at Chandler.
Joanna says, "I see."
Joanna begins, "I think..." Chandler raises his hand and says, "Look, this has nothing to do with you."
Chandler points toward the doorway and says, "And this isn't Rachel's fault. It's me."
Chandler gestures with open hands, saying, "I have serious, serious problems when it comes to women. I have issues with..."
Chandler counts on his fingers, "...commitment, intimacy,"
Chandler pauses, looking down. Laughter is heard.
Chandler points near his eye and adds, "mascara goop."
Audience laughter peaks. Joanna narrows her eyes slightly.
Chandler opens his hands outward and says, "And I'm really sorry. It's just that this isn't going to work out."
Outside the office, Rachel stands with her arms crossed, nodding and clapping her hands together briefly.
Joanna lowers her gaze briefly, then looks back at Chandler and says, "Well, this isn't how I was hoping this would end."
Joanna continues, "But I guess I have to appreciate your honesty." Chandler nods and replies softly, "Yeah."
Chandler says, "Okay."
Joanna looks at Chandler, pressing her lips together.
Chandler nods, points a finger at her, and begins to turn toward the doorway.
Chandler backs out of the room, speaking rapidly at a high volume, "This is great. I'll give you a call. We should do it again sometime." Audience laughter rises.

Rachel covers her face with her hands. The screen fades to black, displaying "EXECUTIVE PRODUCERS KEVIN S. BRIGHT MARTA KAUFFMAN DAVID CRANE" in white text. The scene changes to an apartment, where Joey is holding a cordless phone. White text reads "creative consultant Richard Rosenstock".
Joey paces slightly and says, "So anyway, Beth, what I'm saying is..."
He continues, "...I should have considered your feelings before I went home with you that night." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey runs his free hand over his head. On-screen text reads "coordinating producer Wendy Knoller".
Joey says, "I've recently learned what it's like to be on your side of it. And I'm sorry." The text updates to list associate producers and story editors.
Joey lowers his head slightly. The text updates to "executive story editor Seth Kurland".
Joey asks, "So do you think you can forgive me?" The text lists story editors.
Joey nods slowly. The text reads "guest starring Alison LaPlaca as Joanna".
Joey smiles and says, "Great."
Joey says, "Thanks."
Joey says, "Okay, bye." The text reads "Dina Meyer as Kate".
Joey pushes a button on the phone and flips through a small Rolodex on a nearby surface. The text updates to list the director and another cast member.
Joey lifts the phone back to his ear. The text lists more cast and crew members.
Joey says, "Hello?"
Joey says, "Jennifer?"
Joey continues, "Oh, hi, Mrs. L'Oreal. Is Jennifer there? She's not?" On-screen text lists casting directors.
Joey looks downward. The text lists costume and makeup crew.
Joey says, "Well, actually, I kind of need to talk to you." The text lists technical and property crew.
Joey pauses, then says, "Too."
Audience laughter begins as Joey holds the phone to his ear. The text lists post-production crew.
Joey adds, "Close?" Audience laughter grows louder.
The screen fades to black. Audience laughter fades.
A Warner Bros. Television copyright screen is displayed in white text against a black background.
